title: The Importance of Digital Privacy
description: For questions 17 – 24, read the text below. Use the word given in capitals at the end of some of the lines to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: The (0) security of personal data has become one of the most pressing issues of the digital age. Every time we use an app or browse the internet, we leave digital traces that can reveal details about our lives. Without proper safeguards, this data can fall into (17) dangerous hands and be misused in various ways. Sharing personal details online always carries a (18) risky element, even when websites appear trustworthy. Companies often engage in large-scale (19) collection of user data to improve their services or sell it to advertisers. In many countries, selling data without consent is (20) illegal and subject to heavy fines. Businesses that act in an (21) irresponsible way with customer information risk losing trust and facing serious legal action. Every individual has a right to (22) privacy, and this right must be respected by all organisations. Stricter (23) regulation of data companies is needed to ensure citizens are properly protected. Governments are working to strengthen the legal (24) protection of individuals' digital information.

example.text: The (0) SECURITY of personal data has become one of the most pressing issues of the digital age. (SECURE)

questions.explanation: An adjective is needed before 'hands'; 'danger' becomes 'dangerous' by adding the suffix '-ous'.
An adjective is needed before 'element'; 'risk' becomes 'risky' by adding the suffix '-y'.
A noun is needed after 'large-scale'; 'collect' becomes 'collection' by adding the suffix '-ion'.
A negative adjective is needed; the prefix 'il-' is added to 'legal' to mean 'not permitted by law'.
A negative adjective is needed; the prefix 'ir-' is added to 'responsible' to mean 'not acting carefully'.
A noun is needed after 'right to'; 'private' becomes 'privacy' by changing '-vate' to '-vacy'.
A noun is needed after 'Stricter'; 'regulate' becomes 'regulation' by replacing '-ate' with '-ation'.
A noun is needed after 'legal'; 'protect' becomes 'protection' by adding the suffix '-ion'.
